$comment: FINALIZE TODO ($id): replace the 'yasuilab.example' placeholder host with the SAME host used by the sibling als_sidecar.schema.json so the two ids share a namespace. One-line swap; kept as placeholder here because the sibling's published location was not in hand at finalize time (2026-06-12).
title: trigger_sync sidecar
description: Per-acquisition cross-modal alignment metadata. The injection time-zero (t0) is the recorded injector TTL edge in the Data Recorder HDF5 -- scan-mode independent (sync_architecture.md s2, 2026-06-04). Injection is correlated to imaging via 'anchor' (frame_clock for galvo/resonant; ALS data-file timing for ALS), NEVER via recorder-start: the recorder (Auto Start) begins before the first frame, so head_pad_s is non-zero (recorder-start != frame-0). Metadata-over-filenames: scan parameters live here, not in filenames. Sibling of als_sidecar.schema.json. FINALIZED to the galvo+ALS 2-mode contract (2026-06-12): resonant remains a reserved enum value (hardware absent) and is handled by the else-branch like galvo until its own then-branch is added. The single conformant emitter is make_trigger_sync.py (als_inject_align.py --emit-sidecar delegates to it); schema_version is const-pinned (2026-06-19) so a stale emitter fails loudly. 0.3.0 (C2, method A): adds the required 'recorded_channels' full-inventory block so the HDF5 is self-describing for behavior + sync; the method-B behavior_sidecar is deprecated.

schema_version.description: Version of this sidecar's schema. PINNED to the current contract version with 'const' (2026-06-19, R1 lesson): a stale emitter that writes an older string FAILS validation loudly rather than slipping through a lax pattern. Bump this const TOGETHER with the schema when the contract changes (and update make_trigger_sync.SCHEMA_VERSION in lockstep). History: 0.1.0 -> 0.2.0 at the galvo+ALS 2-mode finalize (adds the required als_datafile_timing block for ALS; non-null als_datafile for ALS). 0.2.0 -> 0.3.0 (C2, method A): adds the required 'recorded_channels' array + optional 'n_samples'; behavior (AI4/AI5) now lives in the SAME Data Recorder HDF5 as injector(AI7)/clock(AI6), so the sidecar documents ALL channels.

scan_mode.description: Imaging scan mode for this acquisition. Lives in metadata, not the filename. 'resonant' is reserved (hardware absent); until a resonant then-branch exists it is validated by the else-branch alongside galvo (frame_clock anchor).

samplerate_hz.description: Data Recorder sample clock (Hz), read from the HDF5 root '/' attribute 'samplerate'. All recorded channels share this single vDAQ clock. Time of any sample = sample_index / samplerate_hz.

n_samples.description: Samples per channel (longest recorded dataset). Optional cross-check; duration_s = n_samples / samplerate_hz.

datarecorder_file.description: Data Recorder HDF5 filename, relative to raw/. Shares the imaging acquisition stem.

als_datafile.description: ALS data stem (no extension, e.g. 'sub-..._00001') for readLineScanDataFiles. REQUIRED and non-null when scan_mode == 'als' (enforced in the ALS then-branch); MUST be null otherwise.

signals.description: Semantic role -> dataset-name pointers used by the t0/anchor logic. The full physical inventory (AI, range) is in 'recorded_channels'; 'signals' just names which dataset plays each timing role. signals.behavior is superseded by recorded_channels entries with role 'locomotion_*' (kept for back-compat).

injector_ttl.description: HDF5 dataset name carrying the injector TTL (Recorded Name), e.g. 'Legato130_TTL'.

frame_clock.description: HDF5 dataset name for the frame clock. Non-null string for galvo/resonant (the D0.0->AI6 loopback); MUST be null for ALS (no per-frame clock dataset -- ALS cycle timing comes from als_datafile_timing's branch-A clock, not this dataset). Enforced per-mode in then/else. NOTE: the frame_clock dataset is still PHYSICALLY recorded for ALS and is listed in recorded_channels with role 'cycle_clock'; this null is a semantic statement, not a claim the dataset is absent.

behavior.description: Optional behavior channel dataset names (treadmill_dir, treadmill_speed, reward, ...). Same Data Recorder HDF5, same vDAQ clock. SUPERSEDED by recorded_channels (role 'locomotion_*'); kept for back-compat.

recorded_channels.description: Full physical inventory of the Data Recorder HDF5: every recorded dataset, self-describing (AI / role / measured range). Mode-independent -- the frame_clock dataset is listed here with role 'cycle_clock' even for ALS (where signals.frame_clock is null). recorded_channels = what is PHYSICALLY recorded; signals = semantic roles for the t0/anchor logic. Added 0.3.0 (C2, method A).

role.description: Controlled vocab; extend ADDITIVELY (e.g. add 'pupil' here and in the emitter ROLE_MAP together).

anchor.description: How injection is correlated to imaging. 'frame_clock' (galvo/resonant) = anchor t0 to the frame_clock pulse train. 'als_datafile_timing' (ALS) = common acq-start + ALS branch-A cycle clock. NEVER anchor to recorder-start: head_pad_s is non-zero.

physical_delay_offset_s.description: Calibrated constant offset (tube dead volume, pump rise) ADDED DOWNSTREAM to t0; NOT folded into t0. null until calibrated.

t0.description: Injection time-zero = the recorded TTL edge. This is analysis time-zero. Scan-mode independent (same for galvo/als/resonant).

source.description: Canonical t0 source (sync_architecture.md s2, 2026-06-04). Scan-mode independent.

dataset.description: Dataset the edge was detected in (matches signals.injector_ttl).

edge.description: Edge polarity used as t0 (rising for a 0->high injector pulse).

threshold_v.description: Threshold for edge detection; midpoint of the observed range (~2.5 V for 5 V logic).

sample_index.description: Index of the t0 edge in the dataset.

t0_recorder_s.description: Recorder-relative edge time = sample_index / samplerate_hz. Analysis time-zero. NOTE: recorder-start != frame-0; correlate to imaging via 'anchor', not this value.

n_edges.description: Sanity count of detected edges; expect 1 for a single pulse (diag_ttl.py).

frame_timebase.description: Frame timing from frame_clock (galvo/resonant). Present ONLY for galvo/resonant; forbidden for ALS (use als_datafile_timing).

n_frames.description: Frame-clock rising edges; should equal the commanded frame count.

rate_hz.description: Frame rate from the MEDIAN inter-frame interval (robust to head/tail padding). Do NOT compute as n_frames / total_duration.

head_pad_s.description: Recorder-start to first frame. Empirically non-zero (~2 s; Auto-Start + scanner/shutter warmup). Proof that recorder-start != frame-0.

inject_frame.description: Frame index the injection edge fell in (frame-anchored). Use this, not t0_recorder_s, to correlate injection with imaging.

inject_offset_into_frame_ms.description: Time from the inject_frame's start to the t0 edge.

als_datafile_timing.description: ALS cycle timing -- the ALS analogue of frame_timebase. Present ONLY for ALS; forbidden for galvo/resonant. Each ALS cycle is an internal 'frame': the existing D0.0->AI6 frame-clock loopback emits one edge per cycle ('branch A clock', als_inject_align.py). recorder-start != cycle-0, so head_pad_s (the ALS 'residual') is non-zero and VARIABLE run-to-run (~2.2-3.5 s) -- it MUST be measured per grab from the branch-A clock, never assumed constant. Deviation from frame_timebase: tail_pad_s is OPTIONAL here (ALS residual work anchors on the head).

source.description: ALS cycle timing comes from the branch-A clock = D0.0->AI6 frame-clock loopback, which emits one rising edge per ALS cycle (als_inject_align.py).

n_cycles.description: Branch-A clock rising edges = ALS cycles detected. A boundary off-by-one vs the commanded cycle count (e.g. 49999 vs 50000) is acceptable ([OK] in als_inject_align).

n_cycles_commanded.description: Commanded cycle count (framesPerSlice x numSlices x numVolumes) for cross-check against n_cycles. Optional.

cycle_rate_hz.description: Cycle rate from the MEDIAN inter-cycle interval of the branch-A clock (e.g. 166.67 Hz for a 6.0 ms cycle). Do NOT compute as n_cycles / total_duration.

head_pad_s.description: Recorder-start to first cycle = the ALS 'residual'. Non-zero and VARIABLE per grab (~2.2-3.5 s). Measured from the branch-A clock every acquisition; never assumed constant.

tail_pad_s.description: Last cycle to recorder-stop. Optional for ALS.

inject_cycle.description: Cycle index the injection edge fell in (cycle-anchored, 1-based; an edge ON a boundary belongs to the cycle it STARTS = searchsorted(edges,idx,'right')). Use this, not t0_recorder_s, to correlate injection with imaging. For the deterministic fixed-frame mechanism this should equal the commanded N+1 across runs (gate 2 PASS).

inject_offset_into_cycle_ms.description: Time from the inject_cycle's start to the t0 edge.
